questions.text: After a long week, you recharge best by…

options.label: Quiet solo time, books, or deep focus
A lively night out with friends
A small dinner with one or two people
Something spontaneous—whatever sounds fun

questions.text: In a new group, you usually…

options.label: Listen first and join when you have something meaningful
Jump in, break the ice, keep energy high
Warm up slowly then open up
Scan the room for interesting people to meet

questions.text: When solving a problem, you prefer…

options.label: Thinking it through alone before sharing
Talking it out loud with others immediately
Draft ideas solo, then refine in discussion
A fast brainstorm with the team

questions.text: Notifications and social energy feel like…

options.label: A drain unless you curate them carefully
Fuel—you like staying connected
Fine in bursts, then you need quiet
Exciting when something fun is happening
Deep work alone on something creative
Hosting, events, or group activities
Collaborating with a trusted partner
Trying something new with a crowd
Withdraw to process feelings privately
Reach out and talk it through with people
Alternate between solitude and one close friend
Stay busy and social to reset your mood

questions.text: At work or school, you shine when…

options.label: You have uninterrupted focus time
You can present, pitch, and energize others
You balance solo work with collaboration
The room needs momentum and morale
Limited—quality over quantity
High—you gain energy from people
Situational—depends on the vibe
Rechargeable quickly with fun plans

results.title: The Deep Diver
results.subtitle: Reflective · Focused · Intentional
results.description: You recharge in quiet and bring depth, clarity, and careful observation. Your best ideas often arrive when the noise fades.
weaknesses: Large crowds can drain you fast
suggestions: Share thinking earlier with trusted people

results.title: The Social Spark
results.subtitle: Expressive · Energizing · Connected
results.description: You light up around people and turn ideas into momentum. Collaboration and conversation help you think clearly and feel alive.
weaknesses: Silence might feel uncomfortable at first
suggestions: Sales, teaching, event roles, community building
Pair social time with a simple wind-down ritual
